data.topic: employee data location and processing rules

data.conclusion: Validated: three-way data residency structural impossibility confirmed. DPDP effective July 1, 2026.

data.topic: employee data location and processing rules

data.conclusion: Bangalore engineering team (240 FTEs) and EU remote workers affected. DPDP enforcement in 19 days.
DPDP enforcement begins July 1, 2026 — 19 days. Non-compliance risks Indian Data Protection Board investigation and GDPR cross-border transfer violations.

assessment.reasoning: IT Security mandates US-only server processing. DPDP requires Indian-jurisdiction processing for Indian-resident employees effective July 1, 2026. HR Remote Work permits those employees to work globally. All three rules apply simultaneously to the same 240-person Bangalore population. DPDP enforcement begins in 19 days.
potential_consequences: Indian Data Protection Board investigation under DPDP Act 2023 (effective July 1, 2026).
GDPR cross-border transfer violation for EU employees.
Engineering data pipeline redesign required before deadline.

data.conclusion: Implement geo-fenced data processing with India-region and EU-region carve-outs. Amend IT Security Policy §4.2. CISO and Legal co-sign before June 30, 2026.

data.title: Data residency: structural impossibility
data.affected: Bangalore engineering team (240 FTEs) and EU remote workers. DPDP effective July 1, 2026.
data.resolution: Implement geo-fenced data processing. Amend IT Security Policy §4.2 with jurisdictional carve-outs. CISO and Legal co-sign before June 30, 2026.

data.topic: employee reporting channels and anonymity guarantees

data.query: employee reporting channels and anonymity guarantees

citations.passage: Reports filed through the ethics portal are anonymous. Employee identity is never logged or traceable by any internal party.
citations.topic: employee reporting channels and anonymity guarantees

citations.passage: All system access is logged with full user identity for security audit purposes. No exceptions permitted.
citations.topic: employee reporting channels and anonymity guarantees

data.topic: employee reporting channels and anonymity guarantees

data.conclusion: Validated: the ethics portal is a system access event. Identity logging and anonymity cannot both apply.

data.topic: employee reporting channels and anonymity guarantees
data.conflict: Anonymity guarantee vs mandatory identity logging

data.conclusion: All ethics portal users affected. Anonymity promise unenforceable. Retroactive exposure risk for historic reports.

data.category: Security Risk (whistleblower identity exposed via audit logs)

data.conclusion: The anonymity guarantee exists on paper. It does not exist in the system. Surprise finding.

assessment.reasoning: Whistleblower Policy §4.2 guarantees anonymity. IT Security Policy §9.3 mandates full identity logging with no exceptions. Ethics portal access is a system access event. These cannot simultaneously be satisfied.
potential_consequences: Identity exposure of whistleblowers in any audit or legal discovery.
Retroactive exposure of historic reports through audit logs.
Legal liability for false anonymity promise.

data.title: Anonymity guarantee vs mandatory identity logging
data.affected: All ethics portal users. Anonymity promise is technically unenforceable.
data.deadline: Immediate — every new report compounds exposure
data.resolution: Deploy third-party anonymized reporting channel outside corporate IT perimeter. Named exception in IT Security §9.3 for ethics portal approved by CISO. HR Compliance and CISO co-own within 30 days.

citations.passage: Reports filed through the ethics portal are anonymous. Employee identity is never logged or traceable by any internal party.
citations.topic: employee reporting channels and anonymity guarantees

citations.passage: All system access is logged with full user identity for security audit purposes. No exceptions permitted.
citations.topic: employee reporting channels and anonymity guarantees

risk_assessment.reasoning: The anonymity guarantee exists on paper. It does not exist in the system.

data.title: Anonymity guarantee vs mandatory identity logging
data.affected: All ethics portal users. Anonymity promise unenforceable.

data.conclusion: Whistleblower Policy §4.2 guarantees anonymity. IT Security Policy §9.3 mandates full identity logging with no exceptions. Ethics portal access is a system access event. The anonymity guarantee exists on paper. It does not exist in the system.
